question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with no fileId set so that toFilter() builds an AddressBookFilter with a null fileId, bypassing controller-level checks and causing the @NotNull constraint violation to be surfaced as an error that leaks internal validation details to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated AddressBookQuery requests with limit=0 (unlimited) to trigger Long.MAX_VALUE streaming of all address book entries, causing resource exhaustion on the mirror node and resulting in missing or delayed records exported to other consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user, given that the database contains a hostname string instead of a dotted-decimal IP in the ipAddressV4 field, trigger a server-side DNS resolution via InetAddress.getByName() in toIpAddressV4(), causing the mirror node to export an incorrect or empty ByteString IP address to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that toIpAddressV4() silently swallows UnknownHostException and returns ByteString.EMPTY, causing the exported NodeAddress to contain an empty IP address instead of the real one, leading to incorrect records being streamed to mirror node clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with stake=0 when the underlying AddressBookEntry has a null stake field, because toNodeAddress() only calls setStake() when stake is non-null, causing the proto default of 0 to be exported as an incorrect stake value?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with an empty description string when the AddressBookEntry description is null, because toNodeAddress() skips setDescription() for null values and the proto default empty string is exported, causing incorrect/missing description records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with ByteString.EMPTY memo when the AddressBookEntry memo is null, because toNodeAddress() skips setMemo() for null values and the proto default is exported, causing missing memo data in the exported record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with ByteString.EMPTY nodeCertHash when the AddressBookEntry nodeCertHash is null, because toNodeAddress() skips setNodeCertHash() for null values, causing missing certificate hash data in the exported record and preventing clients from verifying node identity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with an empty RSAPubKey string when the AddressBookEntry publicKey is null, because toNodeAddress() skips setRSAPubKey() for null values, causing missing public key data in the exported record and preventing clients from verifying node signatures?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with a fileId having shardNum or realmNum set to non-zero values (e.g., shard=1, realm=0, file=102), which passes through toFilter() and EntityId.of() without controller-level rejection, causing the service layer to throw an error that leaks the INVALID_FILE_ID message to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any rate limiting or authentication on the getNodes() gRPC endpoint to flood the server with concurrent streaming requests, causing legitimate mirror node consumers to receive delayed or missing address book records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with limit=Integer.MAX_VALUE (max int32 value 2147483647) to force the server to attempt streaming up to 2147483647 records, causing resource exhaustion and preventing correct address book records from being exported to other mirror node clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user open a streaming getNodes() call and never cancel it, and since the cancel handler (disposable::dispose) is only registered when responseObserver is a ServerCallStreamObserver, exploit cases where it is not to cause a subscription leak that holds database resources and starves other consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that toNodeAddress() uses the deprecated setRSAPubKey() method (suppressed with @SuppressWarnings), and if the underlying public key format in the database is not a plain RSA key string, receive an incorrectly formatted public key in the exported NodeAddress record?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress where the memo field is encoded as UTF-8 via ByteString.copyFromUtf8(addressBookEntry.getMemo()), but if the database memo contains non-UTF-8 byte sequences, the exported memo is silently corrupted, causing incorrect records to be exported to mirror node clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with serviceEndpoints where both domainName and ipAddressV4 are set simultaneously (when the database entry has both fields populated), causing ambiguous or incorrect endpoint data to be exported to mirror node clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user receive a NodeAddress with a serviceEndpoint where ipAddressV4 is ByteString.EMPTY and domainName is also empty (when both fields are blank in the database), causing a completely empty ServiceEndpoint to be exported as a valid record, leading to incorrect address book data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the onErrorMap(ProtoUtil::toStatusRuntimeException) error mapping to receive detailed internal error messages (e.g., constraint violation details, entity not found messages) that reveal internal database structure or entity ID schemes of the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery where hasFileId() returns false (no fileId set), causing toFilter() to build an AddressBookFilter with fileId=null, which then fails @NotNull validation and returns INVALID_ARGUMENT, but the error message reveals that fileId is a required field and its internal constraint name?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a valid getNodes() request and then immediately cancel the stream, and if the responseObserver is not a ServerCallStreamObserver instance, cause the reactive Flux subscription to continue running in the background, wasting server resources and potentially causing incorrect state in the address book context?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user, given a database entry where ipAddressV4 contains a string like '127.0.0.1' (loopback), receive a NodeAddress with a loopback IP exported as a valid service endpoint, causing mirror node clients to attempt connections to localhost instead of the real node address?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user, given a database entry where ipAddressV4 contains '0.0.0.0', receive a NodeAddress with an all-zeros IP exported as a valid service endpoint, causing mirror node clients to receive incorrect/unusable node address records?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user, given a database entry where ipAddressV4 contains an IPv6 address string, observe that InetAddress.getByName() resolves it to a 16-byte address instead of the expected 4-byte IPv4 address, causing the exported ipAddressV4 ByteString to contain incorrect data that breaks mirror node client parsing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send multiple concurrent getNodes() requests for the same fileId with limit=0, causing the server to execute multiple unbounded reactive streams simultaneously against the database, leading to resource contention and incorrect or incomplete records being returned to some consumers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with a fileId where fileNum=0 (e.g., shard=0, realm=0, file=0), which passes through EntityId.of() and toFilter() without controller-level rejection, causing the service to throw an error that reveals the set of valid file IDs (101 and 102) through the INVALID_FILE_ID error message?
